title: Ground every quantified or superlative title claim in a real source
principle: A number or superlative in the title is a factual promise. Backing it with a verifiable source before locking the idea protects long-run trust (and survives fact-check culture), which compounds into returning viewers — whereas an ungrounded claim that later proves false burns the channel.
rule: At research time, any title containing a number/$ amount/superlative must carry a claimSource. The research engine (video-idea.checkClaimGrounding) flags ungrounded claims; resolve before locking, don't defer to script time.

source.rationale: Generated by ViewForge from its own IMPROVEMENT-LOG: vid-1's '$40M' title was locked unsourced (2026-06-28) and only verified mid-script. Codifies the fix as a testable strategy. Derives from the same provenance discipline as the strategy library itself.
